content.thinking:
The files already exist and look correct. Let me verify the implementation against all acceptance criteria:

AC1: income < 10,000 → 0 ✓ (line 28-30)
AC2: income = 10,000 → 10,000 * 0.10 = 1,000 ✓ (falls into `income <= 50_000` branch)
AC3: income 10,001-50,000 → income * 0.10 ✓


AC4-AC8: The remaining criteria all check out—the 10% bracket handles amounts up to 50,000, the 25% bracket covers 50,001 to 150,000, and anything above that gets taxed at 40%, with proper validation for negative or zero income values.

content.text: The files already exist. Let me verify correctness and run the tests.

content.content:
PASS .tmp/calculateTax.test.ts
  calculateTax
    ✓ AC1: returns 0 for income of $9,999 (just below 10% bracket) (1 ms)
    ✓ AC1: returns 0 for income of $1 (well below bracket) (1 ms)
    ✓ AC1: returns 0 for income of $9,999.99 (floating-point just below threshold)
    ✓ AC2: returns income * 0.10 for income of exactly $10,000 (bracket starts inclusive)
    ✓ AC3: returns income * 0.10 for income of $10,001 (just above lower boundary)
    ✓ AC3: returns income * 0.10 for income of $30,000 (mid bracket)
    ✓ AC4: returns income * 0.10 for income of exactly $50,000 (still 10% bracket)
    ✓ AC5: returns income * 0.25 for income of $50,001 (first dollar of 25% bracket)
    ✓ AC5: returns income * 0.25 for income of $100,000 (mid bracket) (1 ms)
    ✓ AC6: returns income * 0.40 for income of $150,001 (first dollar of 40% bracket)
    ✓ AC6: returns income * 0.40 for income of $500,000 (well into 40% bracket)
    ✓ AC7: returns income * 0.25 for income of exactly $150,000 (25% bracket ends inclusive)
    ✓ AC8: throws TaxError with code INVALID_INCOME for income of 0 (3 ms)
    ✓ AC8: throws TaxError with code INVALID_INCOME for a negative income (1 ms)
    ✓ AC8: throws TaxError with code INVALID_INCOME for a large negative income

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       15 passed, 15 total
Snapshots:   0 total
Time:        0.483 s, estimated 1 s
Ran all test suites matching /calculateTax/i.
